response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a seasoned full-stack and systems developer with genuine geospatial side-project experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and a strong distributed-systems background at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, but the role's core requirement is production-grade C++ or Rust in a routing/navigation data pipeline context. Her only commercial C++ work (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015) is over a decade old and brief; her skills list omits C++ entirely, and Rust is nowhere on the resume. Without current systems-language proficiency or any routing-tile/navigation pipeline exposure, she falls well short of the minimum technical bar for this position.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development via GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference talk on the GIS stack
Proven experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby using async I/O and high-performance databases)
Strong software-craftsmanship foundation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a consistent record of quality-oriented delivery across many employers
concerns: No current C++ or Rust proficiency — last commercial C++ work was a short stint at NI in 2014–2015, and C++ does not appear in the skills section
No experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance pipelines, or production-grade geospatial data processing at scale
Primary technology stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web, which is orthogonal to the Linux systems-programming environment required by this role
